title: OpenScience packages a local-first AI workbench for scientific research
friendlyTitle: OpenScience is an AI workspace for researchers
summary: OpenScience is positioning itself as an open-source, local-first AI workbench for scientists, with a browser workspace for literature review, code, experiments, figures, citations, and write-ups. It supports multiple model providers, local models, direct scientific database tools, and hundreds of research-oriented skills.
friendlySummary: OpenScience helps scientists use AI for reading papers, writing code, running experiments, making figures, and drafting reports.
whyItMatters: Scientific AI tools are becoming workflow environments rather than simple chat windows. Researchers who need traceability, citations, experiment artifacts, and model choice may benefit from local-first setups, while still needing sandboxing and careful review of generated claims.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This could save researchers time, but they still need to check the results carefully because science needs evidence.
